description: Click this button to upload sales data files (supports Excel, CSV formats). System will automatically detect key fields such as customer ID, time, and amount
Select the time range for analyzing sales data. Recommend selecting 30-90 days to observe complete customer behavior trends
Select target products or product categories for analysis. Multiple selections allowed for comparative analysis
Real-time monitoring of sales performance and key metrics for each product line, including revenue, order volume, conversion rate, etc.
Analyze market trend changes, forecast future sales direction, identify seasonal patterns and cyclical changes
Compare our products with competitors' market performance, identify competitive advantages and disadvantages, formulate differentiation strategies
Comprehensive assessment of product sales health. 100 points represents optimal state, below 60 requires immediate strategy adjustment
Automatically detect abnormal sales conditions (such as sudden declines, insufficient inventory, etc.) and notify relevant personnel in real-time
Analyze conversion rate from browsing to purchase, identify optimization opportunities, improve sales efficiency
Segment customers based on purchase behavior (RFM analysis), formulate precise marketing strategies, enhance customer value
Analyze product portfolio performance, identify star products, problem products, cash cows, and dogs, optimize product strategy
Provide optimal pricing recommendations based on market response and competitor prices, balance profitability and market share
Evaluate ROI of various promotional activities, identify most effective promotion strategies, optimize marketing budget allocation
Forecast demand, avoid stockouts or excess inventory, reduce inventory costs and improve customer satisfaction
Identify seasonal patterns in sales, plan marketing activities and inventory allocation in advance
Track important KPIs such as revenue, gross margin, average order value, repurchase rate, etc., provide management decision support
Use AI to predict sales trends for the next 30-90 days, assist business planning and resource allocation
Analyze contribution of each marketing channel, optimize budget allocation, improve marketing ROI
Calculate Customer Lifetime Value (CLV), formulate customer retention strategies, enhance long-term profitability
Identify product correlations, provide cross-selling recommendations, increase average order value and customer stickiness
Quantify revenue scale, per-customer value, and profit resilience, track revenue health and growth momentum
Monitor new customer acquisition efficiency, cost, and quality, optimize customer growth strategy
Analyze customer churn rate and retention rate, identify high-risk customers, formulate retention strategies
Track customer activity and conversion funnel, optimize user experience and conversion process
Execute customer DNA analysis (RFM + behavior patterns), deeply understand customer characteristics and value distribution
Days since last purchase. Mean shows average repurchase cycle, median reflects typical customer behavior, 25%/75% quantiles mark active/inactive boundaries, variance measures recency dispersion

Total purchase count during observation period. Gap between mean and median reflects presence of high-frequency customers, high variance indicates need for segmented management, quantiles help identify loyalty levels
Average purchase amount per transaction. High variance indicates need for differentiated pricing strategy. Quantile analysis helps set promotion thresholds and VIP standards

Trend indicator of purchase behavior. >0 indicates increasing activity (rising purchase frequency), ≈0 indicates stability, <0 indicates declining activity (possible churn). Used to predict future customer behavior
Aggregate transaction data by different time periods (daily/weekly/monthly/quarterly/yearly), calculate period revenue, transaction count, customer count and other key metrics
Calculate growth rate of each period relative to previous period, identify business growth or decline trends, support period-over-period and year-over-year analysis
Use moving average to smooth short-term fluctuations, more clearly display long-term trends, default to 3-period moving average
Analyze data trend direction (ascending/descending/stable) and strength based on linear regression, provide simple future predictions
Identify seasonal patterns in sales data, help forecast demand peaks and troughs, optimize inventory and marketing plans
Historical cumulative spending. Reflects customer's historical contribution, used to identify important customers. High/medium/low grouping enables differentiated service strategies

RFM comprehensive weighted score (0.3×R + 0.3×F + 0.4×M) standardized to 0-100. Uses 80/20 rule: Top 20% are high-stability VIP customers, Middle 60% are average-stability customers, Bottom 20% are low-stability risk customers. Higher score indicates more stable transactions and higher value contribution
Analyze time trends in sales data, forecast future direction, support strategic planning decisions
Total sales in USD, represents overall company revenue scale. Tracking this metric reveals business growth trends and market size
Average Revenue Per User in USD, reflects average contribution per customer. Rising ARPU indicates increasing customer value, declining ARPU requires pricing strategy review
Customer Lifetime Value (CLV) in USD, estimates total contribution over entire customer lifecycle. Higher CLV indicates higher customer loyalty and value

Comprehensive indicator (0-100%) of whether customers maintain stable transactions. Top 20% are stable transaction customers requiring VIP maintenance; Bottom 20% are high-risk requiring retention strategies
Total number of current active customers, includes all customers with purchases during observation period, excludes churned or dormant customers
Total number of all customers who have ever purchased since business inception, includes active, dormant, churned and all other statuses

Customer acquisition rate for specific time or geographic scale, comparable to previous periods (monthly, quarterly, yearly). Reflects new customer growth or decline rate for Amazon channel products, helps businesses review new customer expansion effectiveness and optimize marketing strategies

Net change ratio of customer pool, calculates percentage of active customers to total customers. Positive value indicates healthy customer pool growth, negative value indicates churn speed exceeds acquisition speed, requiring immediate retention measures

Proportion of continuously active customers during specific period, core indicator of customer loyalty. High retention rate indicates high customer satisfaction, low retention rate requires review of product and service quality
Proportion of customers who stopped purchasing during specific period, complement to retention rate. Rising churn rate is business risk signal, requires immediate activation of retention measures
Customer group with declining purchase frequency or long time since last purchase, includes drowsy (S1) and semi-dormant (S2) customers. Requires focused attention and reactivation strategies
Proportion of core customers (E0) to total customers, represents health of customer base. Higher ratio indicates more concentrated customer value, requires risk balancing
Predicts number of customers about to enter dormant state, based on purchase cycle and behavior pattern analysis. Early identification reduces churn costs
First-time purchasing new customers (N), are driving force of business growth. Requires special attention to increase second purchase rate
Customers with declining purchase frequency (S1), between active and dormant states. Golden reactivation period with highest success rate
Long-term non-purchasing customers (S3), high reactivation cost but still valuable. Requires strong promotions or reactivation strategies

Customer Activity Index (CAI) measures trend of customer purchase behavior activity. Value >0 indicates increasing activity, =0 indicates stability, <0 indicates declining activity. Used to predict future customer behavior and formulate personalized marketing strategies

Measures conversion proportion of customers advancing from first purchase to loyal customer. High conversion rate indicates excellent product and service quality, high customer satisfaction, beneficial for long-term revenue growth

Proportion of successfully reactivated dormant or drowsy customers. Reflects effectiveness of customer retention strategies, high reactivation rate helps reduce customer acquisition costs and improve overall retention rate
Average number of customer purchases during observation period. High-frequency customers typically have higher loyalty and lifetime value, are key relationship maintenance targets
Average interval in days between two purchases. Shorter interval indicates higher customer stickiness, can be used to predict next purchase timing and schedule marketing activities

Shows distribution of all customers' activity index. Left-skewed distribution indicates most customers have low activity requiring incentives, right-skewed distribution indicates overall good customer performance. Can be used to identify abnormal customers and formulate segmentation strategies

Shows conversion funnel from first purchase to loyal customer. '≥5 purchase conversion rate' refers to proportion of customers with 5+ purchases, reflects product stickiness. Stage-by-stage churn analysis helps identify optimization opportunities
Supports simultaneous upload of multiple files (up to 12), system automatically merges all files. Maximum 200MB per file, supports .csv, .xlsx, .xls formats

System automatically detects key fields: customer_id (customer identifier), payment_time (transaction time), lineitem_price (transaction amount). Priority matching for buyer email, purchase date, item price and other common field names
Please ensure uploading at least 3-12 months of sales data to ensure analysis accuracy. System supports Amazon sales reports, Excel, CSV and other formats
System automatically detects customer identifier field, prioritizes email addresses (buyer email, buyer_email, email), then customer_id, customer, buyer_id, user_id, etc.
System automatically detects transaction time field, includes purchase date, payments date, payment_time, date, time, datetime formats
System automatically detects transaction amount field, includes item price, lineitem_price, amount, sales, price, total
Regardless of original field names, system converts to standard variable names: customer_id, payment_time, lineitem_price for convenient subsequent analysis
Click to load and merge all files, auto-detect fields, and display first 100 records preview. Success shows total record count and unique customer count
After confirming data is correct, click to proceed to customer analysis. Must successfully upload data first to continue
Shows first 100 records of uploaded data for format verification. Supports horizontal scrolling to view all fields
Amazon sales report CSV format example, includes required buyer email, purchase date, item price fields
System automatically records source filename for each record, facilitating tracking and debugging
System automatically removes records missing key fields (customer_id, payment_time, lineitem_price) and converts data types
Shows upload processing status messages, including success, failure, warnings. Please read carefully to confirm operation results
Core customers (E0) are the most loyal and valuable customers who require continuous maintenance. Provide VIP services and exclusive offers to maintain high-value customer loyalty
Semi-sleeping customers (S2) have longer periods of inactivity and face higher churn risk. Design retention strategies such as exclusive offers and product recommendations to re-activate them
